Title: Freddie Mercury born
Summary: Freddie Mercury, Queen's lead vocalist and pianist, was born in Stone Town, Zanzibar.

Title: Brian May born
Summary: Brian May, Queen's lead guitarist, was born in Hampton, Middlesex, England.

Title: Roger Taylor born
Summary: Roger Taylor, Queen's drummer, was born in King's Lynn, Norfolk, England.

Title: John Deacon born
Summary: John Deacon, Queen's bassist, was born in Leicester, England.

Title: Smile forms
Summary: Brian May and Roger Taylor form the band Smile, a precursor to Queen, with Tim Staffell.

Title: Freddie Mercury joins the band
Summary: Following Tim Staffell's departure from Smile, Freddie Mercury joins Brian May and Roger Taylor.

Title: Band renamed Queen
Summary: Freddie Mercury proposes the name Queen for the newly reformed band.

Title: Queen's first live performance
Summary: The band, without a permanent bassist yet, plays its first gig as Queen at a college in Truro.

Title: John Deacon joins Queen
Summary: John Deacon joins as bassist, completing the classic Queen lineup.

Title: Queen's classic line-up debut gig
Summary: The definitive four-piece line-up of Mercury, May, Taylor and Deacon performs live for the first time.

Title: Freddie Mercury designs the Queen crest
Summary: Freddie Mercury, a trained graphic designer, creates the band's iconic crest logo combining the members' zodiac signs.

Title: Queen signs with EMI
Summary: Queen signs a recording contract with EMI Records in the UK.

Title: Queen's first US tour begins
Summary: Queen embarks on its first concert tour of North America, supporting Mott the Hoople.

Title: Freddie Mercury's final concert with Queen
Summary: Queen performs what becomes its last concert with Freddie Mercury, in Knebworth, England.

Title: Freddie Mercury reveals AIDS diagnosis publicly
Summary: Freddie Mercury issues a statement confirming he has AIDS, one day before his death.

Title: Freddie Mercury dies
Summary: Freddie Mercury dies at his home in Kensington, London, from bronchopneumonia related to AIDS.

Title: Freddie Mercury's funeral
Summary: A private funeral service for Freddie Mercury, conducted according to Zoroastrian rites, is held in London.

Title: John Deacon retires from music
Summary: John Deacon effectively retires from the music industry following the Party at the Palace performance.

Title: Queen + Paul Rodgers announced
Summary: Brian May and Roger Taylor announce a touring collaboration with vocalist Paul Rodgers.

Title: Queen + Adam Lambert first perform together
Summary: Brian May and Roger Taylor perform with Adam Lambert for the first time, on the televised talent show American Idol.

Title: Queen + Adam Lambert tour announced
Summary: Queen and Adam Lambert announce an ongoing touring partnership.

Title: Bohemian Rhapsody biopic released
Summary: The Freddie Mercury/Queen biographical film Bohemian Rhapsody premieres in cinemas.

Title: Queen released
Summary: Queen releases the album Queen. Debut studio album.

Title: Queen II released
Summary: Queen releases the album Queen II. Second studio album, featuring 'Seven Seas of Rhye'.

Title: Sheer Heart Attack released
Summary: Queen releases the album Sheer Heart Attack. Third studio album, the band's commercial breakthrough.

Title: A Night at the Opera released
Summary: Queen releases the album A Night at the Opera. Fourth studio album, featuring 'Bohemian Rhapsody'.

Title: A Day at the Races released
Summary: Queen releases the album A Day at the Races. Fifth studio album, featuring 'Somebody to Love'.

Title: News of the World released
Summary: Queen releases the album News of the World. Sixth studio album, featuring 'We Will Rock You' and 'We Are the Champions'.

Title: Jazz released
Summary: Queen releases the album Jazz. Seventh studio album, featuring 'Bicycle Race' and 'Fat Bottomed Girls'.

Title: Live Killers released
Summary: Queen releases the album Live Killers. First official live album.

Title: The Game released
Summary: Queen releases the album The Game. Eighth studio album, the band's first US number-one album.

Title: Flash Gordon released
Summary: Queen releases the album Flash Gordon. Soundtrack album for the film Flash Gordon.

Title: Hot Space released
Summary: Queen releases the album Hot Space. Ninth studio album, exploring funk and dance influences.

Title: Greatest Hits released
Summary: Queen releases the album Greatest Hits. First greatest hits compilation, later the UK's best-selling album.

Title: The Works released
Summary: Queen releases the album The Works. Tenth studio album, featuring 'Radio Ga Ga'.

Title: A Kind of Magic released
Summary: Queen releases the album A Kind of Magic. Eleventh studio album, linked to the film Highlander.

Title: Live Magic released
Summary: Queen releases the album Live Magic. Live album compiled from the Magic Tour.

Title: The Miracle released
Summary: Queen releases the album The Miracle. Twelfth studio album.

Title: Innuendo released
Summary: Queen releases the album Innuendo. Thirteenth studio album, the last released in Freddie Mercury's lifetime.

Title: Greatest Hits II released
Summary: Queen releases the album Greatest Hits II. Second greatest hits compilation.

Title: Made in Heaven released
Summary: Queen releases the album Made in Heaven. Final studio album, completed after Freddie Mercury's death.

Summary: Queen releases the album Greatest Hits III. Third greatest hits compilation.
